title: Confirm Muzi Works R1/R1 Neo hardware facts
description: Why this exists: the firmware target must be based on exact hardware facts, not assumptions. What needs to be done: use subagents to research official Muzi documentation, Meshtastic hardware docs, Meshtastic/MeshCore variant data, schematics if available, and Zephyr upstream support. Capture MCU/SoC, LoRa radio, display, buttons, GNSS, PMIC/battery telemetry, BLE/USB/UART, flash/storage, exact pin mappings, board revisions, flashing path, and unresolved uncertainties. Acceptance criteria: all facts have source references; inferred facts are labeled as inference; unresolved facts create follow-up Beads; no board overlay work proceeds on guessed pinout.
notes:
Hardware fact confirmation completed 2026-06-30 with three research tracks: official/vendor/bootloader sources, Meshtastic/MeshCore variant metadata, and Zephyr feasibility.

Primary source baseline:
- Meshtastic firmware commit inspected: 3edc89d087fdb9f5b40d6365ad7b43585dbe79ac.
- MeshCore commit inspected: e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028.
- R1 Neo Meshtastic sources: boards/r1-neo.json and variants/nrf52840/r1-neo/{platformio.ini,variant.h,variant.cpp}.
- R1 Neo MeshCore sources: variants/muziworks_r1_neo/{platformio.ini,variant.h,variant.cpp,R1NeoBoard.cpp,R1NeoBoard.h,target.cpp}.
- External docs found: Meshtastic R1 Neo hardware page; Muzi R1 Neo guide/product page; Muzi bootloader r1_neo_board; RAK4631 docs for original R1 inference.

Confirmed Muzi R1 Neo facts:
- MCU/SoC: nRF52840, Cortex-M4, 64 MHz, LFXO; Meshtastic hw model slug MUZI_R1_NEO, model id 101.
- Radio: Semtech SX1262, not LR11xx; CS P1.10/42, SCK P1.11/43, MOSI P1.12/44, MISO P1.13/45, BUSY P1.14/46, DIO1 P1.15/47, POWER_EN P1.05/37, TCXO via DIO3 at 1.8 V, RF switch via DIO2.
- Reset conflict: Meshtastic defines SX126X_RESET P1.06/38; MeshCore defines P_LORA_RESET as RADIOLIB_NC while variant.cpp labels P1.06 as reset. Follow-up project-LICHEN-ev5b.9 created before final overlay reliance.
- Display: HAS_SCREEN 0/no built-in screen for R1 Neo.
- Button: user button P0.26/26, active high/no pullup in Meshtastic; MeshCore also uses PIN_USER_BTN 26 and USER_BTN_PRESSED=HIGH.
- LEDs: green P1.04/36, blue P0.28/28, active high.
- GNSS: built-in GPS per product docs; UART pins are ambiguous by naming convention but physical pins are P0.24 and P0.25, GPS_EN P1.01/33, PPS P0.02/2. Meshtastic names SERIAL1_RX=25 and TX=24; MeshCore names GPS_RX=24 and GPS_TX=25. Validate direction during hardware smoke test.
- RTC/I2C: RX8130CE at 0x32 in Meshtastic; I2C SDA P0.19/19, SCL P0.20/20.
- Buzzer: P0.03/3.
- Battery/power: VBAT ADC P0.31/31; Meshtastic ADC multiplier 1.667 and 12-bit sense. DCDC_EN_HOLD P0.13/13 must be driven high early; NRF_ON/SOFT_SHUTDOWN P0.29/29 must be driven high to tell the IO controller the nRF is on. MeshCore also tracks BAT_CHG_STATUS P1.02/34.
- USB/flashing: board JSON uses nrfutil at 115200 with 1200-bps touch; Muzi bootloader supports Adafruit UF2/CDC/DFU, UF2 volume R1-Neo. Zephyr partition/UF2 layout must be validated on hardware; follow-up project-LICHEN-ev5b.10 created.

Original Muzi R1 facts:
- No current public full R1 product page/schematic was found. Treat exact original R1 facts as inferred unless hardware is identified.
- Muzi guide/source evidence points users to Meshtastic RAK4631 target; therefore closest source-backed target is RAK4631/WisCore nRF52840 + SX1262.
- RAK4631 source-backed baseline: SX1262 on CS 42, SCK 43, MOSI 44, MISO 45, BUSY 46, DIO1 47, RESET 38, POWER_EN 37, TCXO 1.8 V via DIO3, RF switch via DIO2; GPS UART RX 15/TX 16 and PPS 17 if RAK1910-like GNSS is present; battery ADC PIN_A0/5; optional e-ink/display and external QSPI details are RAK4631/WisBlock assumptions.
- Muzi guide evidence says original R1 main button may require GPIO 31 configuration when using official Meshtastic; that does not come from the RAK4631 variant itself.

Zephyr feasibility:
- Core R1 Neo MVP is feasible on Zephyr v3.7-era APIs: nRF52840, BLE, USB device, GPIO, ADC, UART GNSS, and upstream SX1262 driver/binding are available.
- Closest upstream board is rak4631/nrf52840 for MCU/radio wiring; local LICHEN t_echo_nrf52840 is useful for nRF52840+SX1262+GNSS shape, and t1000_e_nrf52840 is useful for Adafruit UF2-style partition notes.
- R1/R1 Neo are not drop-in upstream boards; custom Zephyr board DTS/defconfig/pinctrl/partition layout is required.

Follow-up Beads created:
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.8: unresolved public schematic and board-revision gaps.
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.9: R1 Neo SX1262 reset pin conflict.
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.10: Muzi/Adafruit bootloader partition and UF2 validation.
- project-LICHEN-ev5b.11: non-Neo display and possible LR1121 support boundary.
close_reason: Completed: hardware facts recorded from Meshtastic/MeshCore/vendor/Zephyr sources; unresolved uncertainty follow-ups created under epic.
